Title: Successful user registration and homepage access
Preconditions: User is not logged in; registration form is accessible.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., `test@example.com`), strong password, matching confirmation.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the registration page.
2. User enters valid email, password, and confirmation.
3. User submits the form.
4. System confirms registration success.
5. User is redirected to the homepage.
Expected Result: User is successfully registered and lands on the homepage.

Title: Failed registration attempt and new user creation prompt
Preconditions: User is not logged in; registration form is accessible.
Test Data: Invalid email (e.g., `test@`), weak password, mismatched confirmation.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the registration page.
2. User enters invalid data (e.g., weak password or mismatched fields).
3. User submits the form.
4. System detects errors and prompts for new user creation.
5. User clicks "Create New User" and re-enters valid data.
6. User submits the form again.
Expected Result: System rejects invalid data, prompts for correction, and allows successful registration upon valid input.

Title: Registered user skips registration check and accesses homepage
Preconditions: User is already registered and logged in.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in with valid credentials.
2. System verifies registration status as "registered."
3. User is automatically redirected to the homepage.
Expected Result: User bypasses registration check and lands on the homepage.

Title: Navigate to homepage via sidebar menu
Preconditions: User is logged in; on the homepage.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the sidebar menu icon.
2. User selects "Homepage" from the menu.
3. System closes the menu and opens the homepage.
Expected Result: User is redirected to the homepage without errors.

Title: Navigate to "Le mie segnalazioni" via sidebar menu
Preconditions: User is logged in; on the homepage.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the sidebar menu icon.
2. User selects "Le mie segnalazioni."
3. System closes the menu and opens the reports page.
Expected Result: User is redirected to the "Le mie segnalazioni" page.

Title: Navigate to "Impostazioni" via sidebar menu
Preconditions: User is logged in; on the homepage.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the sidebar menu icon.
2. User selects "Impostazioni."
3. System closes the menu and opens the settings page.
Expected Result: User is redirected to the "Impostazioni" page.

Title: Invalid menu selection (edge case)
Preconditions: User is logged in; on the homepage.
Test Data: N/A (simulate a broken menu link or disabled option).
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the sidebar menu icon.
2. User attempts to select a non-existent or disabled menu option (e.g., via manual URL manipulation or broken link).
3. System detects the invalid selection.
Expected Result: System either ignores the selection, shows an error, or redirects to a default page (e.g., homepage).

Title: Successful alert creation with valid form data
Preconditions: User is logged in; on the "Add Alert" page.
Test Data: Valid data for the first 4 fields (e.g., title, description, category, location).
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Add Alert" in the sidebar menu.
2. User fills in the first 4 fields correctly.
3. User clicks "Add Category" and selects a valid category.
4. System validates the fields as correct.
5. User clicks the green "Submit" button.
Expected Result: Alert is created successfully; user sees a confirmation message.

Title: Failed alert creation with invalid form data
Preconditions: User is logged in; on the "Add Alert" page.
Test Data: Invalid data for the first 4 fields (e.g., empty title, special characters in description).
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Add Alert" in the sidebar menu.
2. User leaves the first 4 fields blank or enters invalid data.
3. User clicks the green "Submit" button.
4. System detects incomplete/invalid data.
5. User corrects the fields and resubmits.
Expected Result: System rejects submission, highlights errors, and allows correction before successful submission.

Title: Skip category selection and submit alert
Preconditions: User is logged in; on the "Add Alert" page.
Test Data: Valid data for the first 4 fields; no category selected.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Add Alert" in the sidebar menu.
2. User fills in the first 4 fields correctly.
3. User skips selecting a category.
4. User clicks the green "Submit" button.
Expected Result: System rejects submission and requires category selection before proceeding.

Title: View all alerts and navigate to a specific alert detail
Preconditions: User is logged in; at least one alert exists in the system.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "View Alerts" in the sidebar menu.
2. System displays a list of all alerts.
3. User clicks on one alert from the list.
4. System opens the detailed view of the selected alert.
Expected Result: User sees the list of alerts and the detailed view of the clicked alert without errors.

Title: No alerts exist in the system
Preconditions: User is logged in; no alerts exist.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "View Alerts" in the sidebar menu.
2. System detects no alerts are present.
Expected Result: System displays a message indicating "No alerts found" or an empty state.

Title: Navigate to profile and view dwellings
Preconditions: User is logged in; profile has dwelling data.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Profile" in the sidebar menu.
2. User clicks the blue house icon on the profile page.
3. System redirects to the dwellings screen.
Expected Result: User is successfully redirected to the dwellings screen.

Title: Save valid profile changes
Preconditions: User is logged in; on the profile page with editable fields.
Test Data: Valid updated data (e.g., new address, phone number).
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Profile" in the sidebar menu.
2. User edits profile fields with valid data.
3. System validates the fields as correct.
4. User clicks "Save Changes."
Expected Result: Changes are saved successfully; user sees a confirmation message.

Title: Correct invalid profile data before saving
Preconditions: User is logged in; on the profile page with editable fields.
Test Data: Invalid data (e.g., empty required field, invalid phone number).
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Profile" in the sidebar menu.
2. User enters invalid data in profile fields.
3. User clicks "Save Changes."
4. System detects errors and prompts for correction.
5. User corrects the data and resubmits.
Expected Result: System rejects invalid data, highlights errors, and allows saving only after corrections.

Title: Discard profile changes
Preconditions: User is logged in; on the profile page with unsaved changes.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Profile" in the sidebar menu.
2. User makes edits to profile fields.
3. User clicks "Discard Changes."
Expected Result: All unsaved changes are discarded; original data is restored.

Title: Rapid menu clicks during navigation
Preconditions: User is logged in; on the homepage.
Test Steps:
1. User rapidly clicks multiple sidebar menu options (e.g., "Homepage," "Le mie segnalazioni," "Impostazioni") in quick succession.
2. System processes each click.
Expected Result: System handles rapid clicks gracefully; no crashes or duplicate actions occur.

Title: Back button navigation after alert creation
Preconditions: User is on the alert creation page with unsaved data.
Test Steps:
1. User fills out the alert form partially.
2. User clicks the browser back button.
3. User returns to the alert creation page.
Expected Result: System either retains unsaved data or prompts the user to confirm discarding changes.
